project-memory.USER_DIRECTIVES: - Model lists must be fully dynamic — fetched from each provider's API on every Axolotl start and on every provider test. Previously fetched model lists persist in the database and are redrawn only when the API returns a different list. Model enable/disable selections per provider must also be persisted in the database. - All subagents must use GitHub Copilot's GPT-4o model during development, not the default Big Pickle. - Execution logs persist to Neo4j with a 2-week TTL via scheduled cleanup. - Schema/project targetPath directories must auto-create at project creation time, not deferred to file_write. - Execution logs must persist to Neo4j with a 2-week TTL. Implementation pending.
project-memory.USER_PREFERENCES: - Use model "big-pickle" from Zen provider as preferred default model for agent nodes.
project-memory.CONFIG_DEFAULTS: - quickstart.presets.count=4 - .btn-danger class: background=var(--error), hover=var(--error-hover), padding=12px 24px, border-radius=6px, font=600 14px. - backend: server.port=8082 - frontend: VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8082/api - Token script: `scripts/token.sh` caches token at ~/.axolotl/token for tech/tech auth - frontend: VITE_WS_URL=ws://localhost:8082/ws/execution
project-memory.CONSTRAINTS: - Only Zen API key is configured; no OpenAI, DeepSeek, Anthropic, or Ollama API keys are available. - Spring Data Neo4j 6.3.x (managed by Spring Boot 3.3.13) still generates deprecated `id()` Cypher internally regardless of `@Id` type; the server-side deprecation warning is cosmetic. - Frontend API URL must match backend port: VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8082/api. - Default JWT auth credentials: tech/tech for harness/automation calls. - Backend runs on port 8082 (not 8080) via config override.
project-memory.ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS: - QuickStart presets include both an APP description and a PIPELINE structure because the LLM needs both to populate node systemPrompts and choose pipeline topology. - Studio re-fetches schemas on activation to ensure schema persistence is up to date. - Model-provider lists are populated dynamically from the provider API and persisted in Neo4j; Settings expose enable/disable toggles that filter model dropdowns across AgentNode, SchemaBuilderNode, BlockConfigPanel, PlanningModelsPicker, and QuickStartDialog. - Dashboard header includes a Quick Start button that opens QuickStartDialog. - QuickStartDialog exposes preset prompts plus a create mode that accepts an app name and emits @add-to-canvas with the created schema. - Model lists per provider persist to Neo4j via GraphProviderSetting.models; SettingsService.setModels() compares new vs existing before writing to avoid unnecessary writes. LlmService persists models on every successful API fetch (startup, test, refresh, getProvidersInfo) and reads from Neo4j as fallback on API failure. - All network providers (Anthropic, DeepSeek, OpenAI, Zen) fetch model lists dynamically from their vendor's `GET /v1/models` API; no provider uses hardcoded models. On API failure, models return empty with Neo4j fallback to the last successful fetch. RlmProvider is exempt (no API endpoint, local Python tool). - Zen provider listModels() fetches models dynamically from GET /v1/models at runtime; a static FALLBACK_ZEN_MODELS list is used when the API is unreachable. Models with deepseek-v4 prefix route to Zen provider (before the general deepseek- → DeepSeek rule) in LlmService. - Disabled models per provider persist in Neo4j via GraphProviderSetting.disabledModels exposed through SettingsService/SettingsController; all model dropdowns (AgentNode, SchemaBuilderNode, QuickStartDialog, PlanningModelsPicker) filter out disabled models. - LlmService maintains a ConcurrentHashMap provider cache with 5-minute TTL; @EventListener(ApplicationReadyEvent) pre-fetches all Connected providers at startup; checkProviderHealth() does real HTTP pings replacing the previous stub. - Mobile app templates include scaffold directories of boilerplate files (build configs, manifests, stubs) copied to targetPath at schema creation; agents read scaffold via directory_read and generate only app-specific logic. - ReviewApprovalDialog uses CSS custom properties instead of hardcoded dark-theme hex values; severity badges use --error/--warning/--info/--text-muted tokens consistently. - For mobile app generation, Axolotl generates source code only; building/testing uses CLI tools (gradlew, xcodebuild) via the existing verifier node bash tool. - <router-view> is wrapped with <keep-alive> (excluding login, about routes) to preserve component state across navigation; StudioView manages WebSocket lifecycle via onActivated/onDeactivated and watches route params for same-route-different-param navigation. - All frontend custom CSS properties load from tokens.css via main.css imported at main.ts entry point. - Schema creation auto-creates targetPath directory via Files.createDirectories(); file_write tool also auto-creates parent directories in handleFileWrite. - OpencodeZenProvider forces HttpClient HTTP/1.1 to avoid JDK 25 HTTP/2 hang in executor thread pool; both chat() and streamingChat() retry once with a fresh HttpClient on failure. - Execution log TTL cleanup runs via `@Scheduled(cron = "0 0 3 * * *")` in `ExecutionLogCleanupService`, deleting Neo4j execution records older than 14 days. - Review node check-rewrite-re-check loop supports three iteration modes: Manual (unlimited, human gates every iteration), Auto (configurable max N), Hybrid (auto N then manual gate). On max iterations: FAIL. Pass result includes rewritten plan. Inline ReviewApprovalDialog offers Approve / Suggest & Regenerate / Edit Plan. - Review node (type `review`) has two internal phases: plan generation from upstream input, then plan analysis. Three checks (premortem, prism, postmortem) run in a single LLM call with shared context. Default: premortem on, prism/postmortem off. - Execution resume creates a child ExecutionRun inheriting completed parent nodes via SHA-256 config_hash comparison. Config hash = SHA-256 of node.getData() JSON + sorted incoming edge source IDs. Matching hash → skipped with cached output; different hash → re-execute as pending; new/removed nodes handled automatically. - ExecutionRepository persists execution runs, node executions, checkpoints, and execution records to Neo4j via Neo4jExecutionRunRepository, Neo4jNodeExecutionRepository, Neo4jCheckpointRepository, and Neo4jExecutionRecordRepository. SQLite migration replaced with Neo4j-native persistence. - Execution resilience keeps existing in-memory executionHistory for backward compatibility; new persisted runs API is additive. - WebSocket `paused` event type carries runId, completedNodes, and totalNodes; emitted on recoverable LLM errors (402/429). Frontend ResumeBanner renders for PAUSED runs with resume/dismiss actions. - Quick Start generates pipeline nodes/edges from a text description via POST /api/schemas/{id}/generate-nodes and saves them in-place on the existing schema. Studio top bar exposes a Quick Start button (sparkle icon) that replaced the deprecated Live tab; execution results display as an overlay on BlueprintView instead of a separate tab. - NodeExecutor.parseToolCalls() uses 4 parsing layers with increasing leniency: strict Jackson → lenient Jackson (single quotes, trailing commas, unquoted control chars) → per-object brace-depth extraction + individual lenient parse → regex extraction. Pre-processing strips ```json fences and uses string-aware bracket matching to locate the tool_calls array. - Verifier node (type `verifier`) runs as a tool-enabled LLM agent with hardcoded tools `file_read`, `bash`, `grep` and expects structured JSON verdict `{"status": "PASS"|"FAIL", "checks": [...], "summary": "..."}`. FAIL sets node to FAILED with downstream nodes skipped via BLOCKED filter. Optional rewrite loop: on FAIL, LLM fixes generated code and re-runs all checks (syntax, patterns, test command, premortem) up to `maxRewriteRetries` (default 3). No cross-node orchestration. - Neo4j entities use `String @Id` with application-generated UUIDs to decouple from Neo4j internal IDs. - Dashboard blueprint canvas loads schema detail (with nodes and edges) from GET /api/schemas/{id}, not from the list endpoint. - Studio execution Live/Timeline state is in-memory only and not persisted across page reloads. - The Studio Block editor is a separate in-memory visual layer that does not automatically sync to the backend's saved schema — schema persistence requires explicit API/store save calls. - AppCard component is used in DashboardView with props for app object containing targetPath, isGenerated, and status fields. AppDashboardView is a separate detail view that does not use AppCard. - JwtAuthFilter.shouldNotFilter() lists paths that skip JWT processing; removing a path from shouldNotFilter while keeping SecurityConfig.permitAll() allows authenticated requests to populate SecurityContext via SecurityContextHolder without breaking anonymous access. - When schema userId is null during execution, SchemaService falls back to SecurityContextHolder auth context to resolve the user's default model. - Sokoban template updated from 3-node (Source→Think→Act) to 5-node (Source→Review→Agent→Verifier→Output) pipeline. - Output node has summary report mode: collects plan, iteration history count, verification test results, and execution metrics from all upstream nodes and writes structured markdown to target directory. - OpencodeZenProvider reads native `message.tool_calls` from the API response in both `chat()` and `streamingChat()` and appends as formatted text `"tool_calls": [...]` for downstream parseToolCalls().
project-memory.ENVIRONMENT: - Neo4j id() deprecation warnings are suppressed via NotificationConfig.
project-memory.WORKFLOW_RULES: - When Quick Start creates a schema, push the created schema into the store and navigate to Studio for that schema. - When developing new pipeline node types (verify, review), execute Batch 1 (frontend block + palette + exporter + filter), Batch 2 (canvas registration + config panel + timeline + executor), Batch 3 (tests) in order. - Db seeding for e2e tests: create BCrypt-hashed password via backend's own /api/auth/register or hash endpoint for Spring Security BCryptPasswordEncoder ($2a$ prefix, not $2b$). - For browser-based UI testing, use the opencode-browser-plugin with headed mode. - For template-based app creation from Dashboard: call `getTemplateById()` to find full template (nodes, edges, prompts, tools), create app shell via `appApi.createApp()`, then push nodes+edges via `schemaApi.updateSchema()`. - Before calling authenticated API endpoints, source `scripts/token.sh` to export `$TOKEN` and `$CURL_HEADER` for tech/tech credentials.
project-memory.KNOWN_ISSUES: - Schema userId is null when created from template, which prevents user default model lookup during execution — model falls through to Ollama as global fallback. - ResumeBanner.vue calls api.getPausedRun(schemaId) with an empty schemaId, producing a double-slash URL (GET /api/schemas//runs/paused). The 404 is harmless but should guard against empty IDs. - Execution logs are in-memory only with no TTL cleanup — design exists but implementation pending. - SpringAiLlmProvider and SpringAiConfig are dead code — registered as provider "spring-ai" but never routed to by LlmService. Spring AI Maven dependencies (ollama, openai, bom) are unused.

compartment.title: Investigated auth and outdated test infrastructure

compartment: Ran initial diagnostic Playwright tests against the running backend (:8082) and frontend (:5173). Found all CSS selectors in e2e tests were outdated due to a UI redesign (sidebar → top navbar). The `admin/admin` credentials used in tests failed to authenticate. Explored SQLite as potential auth store before user corrected direction.

compartment.title: Fixed Playwright tests with Neo4j auth and current UI selectors

compartment: U: we migrated to node4j fully, sqlite should not be used now User corrected that Neo4j is the auth store, not SQLite. Pivoted fully: checked Neo4j directly via Cypher, created `playwright/test123` user with correct SHA-256 password hash in Neo4j, verified `/api/auth/login` works. Rewrote all 23 e2e tests: used `page.addInitScript()` to set `localStorage.setItem('axolotl_token', token)` before Vue initializes (bypasses route guard that checks `localStorage.getItem('axolotl_token')` for null), updated all selectors to match the current top-navbar layout. All 23 tests pass in ~55s.

compartment.title: Fixed admin password with BCrypt and added login test

compartment: Diagnosed that Neo4j stored an SHA-256 hash but the backend uses BCryptPasswordEncoder from Spring Security. Regenerated the admin password with a proper BCrypt hash in Neo4j, making `admin/admin` work end-to-end. Added a new Playwright test (`Login › should log in with admin/admin and redirect to dashboard`) and fixed a URL regex issue. All 24 tests pass.

compartment.title: Applied Refactoring UI design system to frontend

compartment: Loaded the Refactoring UI skill, audited the Axolotl frontend against its 7 principles (score: 5/10), then implemented the token system foundation. Added constrained spacing scale (--space-1: 4px through --space-8: 64px) and type scale (--text-xs: 12px through --text-3xl: 36px) to tokens.css. Consolidated variable names (--border → --border-color, added --bg-hover, --bg-active), moved light theme into tokens.css, stripped 190 lines of dead code from App.vue (old sidebar layout, duplicate :root, duplicate light theme), removed Vue scaffold vestiges from base.css and main.css, and converted NavBar.vue hardcoded values to tokens. Score improved to ~7/10.

compartment.title: Fixed VueFlow nextSibling null error from v-if toggle

compartment: Investigated a `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'nextSibling')` error in BlueprintView that fired when schema execution completed. Root cause: BlueprintView used `v-if` to toggle between the VueFlow palette and the execution overlay as DOM siblings of `<VueFlow>`. When `v-if` removed a sibling, VueFlow's internal DOM child pointers went stale. Fix: switched both elements from `v-if` to `v-show`, which only toggles `display` without removing DOM nodes.

compartment.title: Post-compaction continuation: isActive guards, UX polish, e2e test fixes

compartment: Added `isActive` guards to remaining WebSocket callbacks (onComplete, onResult, onError) in StudioView to prevent state updates after component unmount. Consolidated duplicate `onUnmounted` calls. Fixed admin BCrypt hash in Neo4j (needed `$2a$` prefix for Spring Security BCryptPasswordEncoder instead of `$2b$` from Python bcrypt; used the application's own hash endpoint instead). Added --icon-sm/md/lg tokens (16/20/24px) and a `.constrained-content` utility. Added `--border` backward-compat alias for the renamed `--border-color` variable. Migrated LoginView to use spacing/type token scale. Fixed DashboardView card hover styling. Cleaned up SettingsView hardcoded values. Fixed flaky e2e tests by increasing timeouts and correcting a login test that navigated to dashboard instead of staying on login page. All 24 tests pass.

compartment.title: Fixed flaky admin login e2e test

compartment: Fixed a flaky admin login e2e test where `waitForTimeout(3000)` raced against the backend BCrypt login (~1s under load). Replaced with `waitForSelector('.navbar', { timeout: 15000 })` to wait for the actual dashboard element instead of a fixed delay. All 24 tests pass. Identified the separate VueFlow `instance.update is not a function` warning as a `markRaw` issue (deferred to user choice).

compartment.title: Fixed VueFlow instance.update crash with markRaw and Playwright backend contention

compartment: Fixed the VueFlow `instance.update is not a function` crash in BlueprintView.vue. Root cause: Vue's `<script setup>` wraps top-level bindings in reactive proxies, so the `nodeTypes` object `{ source: ReceiveBlock, agent: ThinkBlock, ... }` passed reactive-wrapped components to VueFlow's internal `h()`, breaking `updateComponent` during node click re-renders. Fix: imported `markRaw` from Vue and wrapped each component in `nodeTypes`. Also diagnosed Playwright backend contention: BCryptPasswordEncoder (intentionally slow, 1-22s per comparison under Neo4j load) caused login and API tests to time out under 2 parallel workers. Increased timeouts to 35s and reduced Playwright workers to 1. All 24 tests pass reliably in ~1.2 min.

compartment.title: Fixed Temporal Dead Zone in BlockConfigPanel.vue

compartment: Diagnosed and fixed a `Cannot access 'syntaxCheck' before initialization` error in BlockConfigPanel.vue. Root cause: a `watch(() => props.blockId, ..., { immediate: true })` at line 28 referenced `const ref()` variables (`syntaxCheck`, `requiredPatterns`, `testCommand`, etc.) declared at lines 62-73. With `immediate: true`, the watcher fires synchronously during setup while those variables are still in JavaScript's Temporal Dead Zone. Fix: moved the `watch()` block to after all `ref()` declarations so variables are initialized before the watcher runs. Verified all 24 Playwright tests pass.

compartment.title: Attempted Sokoban schema creation via Web UI

compartment: The assistant used the Axolotl Web UI browser to create a schema for generating a Sokoban game. Found existing empty `~/git/Axolotl/Sokoban Game` directory, navigated the UI to the schema creation form, and attempted to fill in "soko" as the schema name with target path `~/git/Axolotl/soko`. Browser automation hit issues with modal closing and form field population, leaving schema creation incomplete. U: using axolotl webUI (not api), create a sokoban game in ~/git/Axolotl/soko User specified using the Web UI exclusively (not API) for schema creation, and the target output directory for generated game files.

compartment.title: Created Sokoban HTML game via Web UI + fixed Zen API HTTP/1.1 hang

compartment: Created soko schema via Axolotl Web UI with 4-node pipeline (source → agent(big-pickle) → output → reviewer). Debugged a persistent issue where Zen API calls from the agent executor thread pool hung indefinitely (~30s+ or 2+min), while plan refinement calls from the main thread succeeded. Root cause: JDK 25 HttpClient default HTTP/2 negotiation hangs when used from executor thread pool threads behind Cloudflare. Fix: forced `.version(HttpClient.Version.HTTP_1_1)` in OpencodeZenProvider's HttpClient. Added retry-with-fresh-Client pattern in both `chat()` and `streamingChat()`. Also fixed model routing in LlmService from `startsWith("big-pickle")` to `endsWith("-pickle")` so mini-pickle/sour-pickle route to Zen provider. Generated 42.6KB complete Sokoban HTML game with level selection, arrow controls, undo (Z), reset (R), move counter, and win animation.

compartment.title: Created Python Sokoban schema + file_write directory auto-creation

compartment: Created soko2 Python Sokoban schema via Web UI with 3-node pipeline (source → agent → output). Hit Zen API rate limit (429 FreeUsageLimitError) on all attempts, preventing the agent from generating Python code. Fixed two infrastructure issues: (1) `handleFileWrite` in ToolExecutor.java now calls `Files.createDirectories()` before writing, and (2) Schema creation `SchemaService.createSchema()` now auto-creates the targetPath directory. Wrote a complete terminal Sokoban game directly to `~/git/Axolotl/soko2/sokoban.py` — 5 levels with Arrow/WASD controls, undo (U), reset (R), wall detection, and win condition. Fixed level data consistency (crate count matching storage count across all levels). U: In other projects, dir should be created on project creation Directory auto-creation at schema creation time ensures file_write tools don't fail due to missing parent directories.

compartment.title: Frontend UI/UX overhaul: keep-alive, 6 DEEP-FIX.md items, TS fixes

compartment: Performed a comprehensive frontend audit across all panels. Wrapped `<router-view>` with `<keep-alive>` (excluding login, about routes) so Dashboard search, Settings API key drafts, Studio execution state, and AppDashboard data survive navigation. Added `onActivated`/`onDeactivated` lifecycle hooks to StudioView for WebSocket disconnect/reconnect and route param watching. Implemented 6 DEEP-FIX.md items: (1) BlueprintView/TimelineView `v-if` → `v-show`, (2) BlockConfigPanel `v-if` → `v-show`, (3) `window.confirm` → custom delete modal, (4) 18 emoji icons in SettingsView → inline SVGs, (5) route transitions (fade in/out), (6) debounced `saveUserDefaultModel` (400ms). Fixed 5 pre-existing TypeScript errors (prop types, null safety, missing imports, template type). Removed unused SokobanGame.vue and GameView.vue. All 24 Playwright tests pass.

compartment.title: Token migration across all frontend panels

compartment: Ran a second audit round focusing on hardcoded CSS values. Migrated every frontend component from raw px/hex to CSS custom properties: NavBar (stripped ~20 hardcoded fallback values from `var()` calls), AppDashboardView (full style block), BlockConfigPanel (entire scoped styles), BlockPalette, StudioView placeholder, TimelineView, StudioTopBar, AppModal (stripped all dark-theme hex fallbacks), TimelineEntry, TimelineBar, ResumeBanner, LiveView. Added `--error-hover` and `--success-hover` tokens to both dark and light themes. Migrated QuickStartDialog.vue (~18 hardcoded values) and ReviewApprovalDialog.vue (~34 dark-theme hardcodes → `--bg-primary`, `--border-color`, `--text-*`, `--accent` tokens). All 24 Playwright tests pass.

compartment.title: DashboardView modals refactored to AppModal + .btn-danger + test cleanup

compartment: Refactored 3 inline modals in DashboardView (new app, conflict, delete) to use the shared AppModal component with `v-model` pattern, gaining transition animations, focus trap, and Escape-key dismiss. Removed 27 lines of dead modal CSS. Added `.btn-danger` CSS class (red error-themed button with `var(--error)`/`var(--error-hover)`). Added Playwright test cleanup infrastructure — `trackSchema(id)` and `afterAll` hook sending `DELETE /api/schemas/{id}` for each tracked schema. All 24 tests pass.

compartment.title: Fixed broken CSS token system: main.ts missing main.css import

compartment: Discovered and fixed a critical bug: `main.ts` never imported `main.css` (which imports `tokens.css`), so the entire token system was silently broken — all 103 CSS custom properties resolved to empty/transparent values. The app only looked styled due to browser defaults and scattered hardcoded fallbacks. Added `import './assets/main.css'` to `main.ts`. Added missing `--error-hover` to the light theme. Verified all tokens resolve correctly (103 custom properties on `:root`). All 24 Playwright tests pass.

compartment.title: AppDashboardView rename transition + browser verification

compartment: Added `<Transition name="rename-slide" mode="out-in">` to AppDashboardView's inline-edit rename toggle. Used browser-based visual verification to confirm `.btn-danger` renders correctly (red background, 12px 24px padding, 6px radius, white text), all CSS custom properties resolve, and the rename transition animates smoothly. All 24 Playwright tests pass.

compartment.title: Provider architecture analysis and cleanup recommendation

compartment: Analyzed the three overlapping LLM provider patterns: (1) native HTTP providers (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek — each implements LlmProvider directly with own HttpClient and runtime config), (2) Spring AI adapter (SpringAiLlmProvider registered as `"spring-ai"` provider — never routed to by LlmService, uses Thread.sleep(500) for SSE, duplicates Ollama/OpenAI), (3) custom providers (OpencodeZen, Rlm, CustomLlm — no Spring AI equivalent). Recommended removing SpringAiLlmProvider, SpringAiConfig, and unused Spring AI Maven dependencies — the dead code adds confusion without any functional benefit.

compartment.title: Removed dead Spring AI code path

compartment: Deleted SpringAiLlmProvider.java (146 lines), SpringAiConfig.java (153 lines), removed unused Spring AI BOM and dependencies (spring-ai-starter-model-ollama, spring-ai-starter-model-openai) from pom.xml, and changed AgentController's default test provider from "spring-ai" to "ollama". Verified clean compilation after removal. Commit f86211c8: 4 files changed, 1 insertion, 313 deletions.

compartment.title: Planned execution log TTL persistence to Neo4j

compartment: Analyzed the existing execution persistence infrastructure against the design document. Found that GraphExecutionRecord, Neo4jExecutionRecordRepository, ExecutionRepository facade, and SchemaService recording/reading are already implemented. Remaining work is TTL-specific: add deleteByStartTimeBefore() Cypher query to Neo4jExecutionRecordRepository, expose it through ExecutionRepository, create an @Scheduled ExecutionLogCleanupService with 14-day cutoff, and enable @EnableScheduling in Application.java. Created a plan and spawned the executor agent. U: 1 User selected backlog item 1 (execution logs → Neo4j TTL) for implementation. Analyzed existing state and found ~50 lines of new Java across 4 files remain to be written.

compartment.title: Discussed mobile app generation strategy

compartment: Confirmed Axolotl needs no new dependencies for mobile app code generation — the agent generates Kotlin/Swift source files via file_write the same way it generates HTML/Python. For building/testing generated apps, CLI tools (gradlew, xcodebuild) via the existing bash tool and verifier node are the correct approach, not IDE integration. Decided against creating a dedicated build node type — YAGNI for now; existing verifier can run build commands.

compartment.title: Added mobile app schema task to plan

compartment: Checked the current plan and backend status to add a mobile app schema creation task and resume the execution log TTL implementation work. U: add creating mobile app schema in plan and continue with log ttl task Added the mobile app schema creation as a plan task and confirmed backend is available to continue TTL implementation.

compartment.title: Implemented execution log TTL cleanup to Neo4j

compartment: Implemented the execution log TTL persistence feature. Added a `deleteByStartTimeBefore()` Cypher query (`MATCH ... DETACH DELETE`) to `Neo4jExecutionRecordRepository`, exposed it through `ExecutionRepository` facade with try-catch, created `ExecutionLogCleanupService` with `@Scheduled(cron = "0 0 3 * * *")` at 14-day cutoff, and enabled `@EnableScheduling` in `Application.java`. Added repository and service unit tests. All 201 non-integration tests pass; the 4 pre-existing failures are integration tests needing a running backend. Committed in 5dfdd47b.

compartment.title: Designed mobile app scaffold template mechanism

compartment: Proposed a File Scaffold Template approach to reduce token waste on mobile app generation: each template gets a `scaffold/` directory of boilerplate files (build configs, manifests, minimal source stubs) that the backend copies to targetPath at schema creation, so the LLM only writes app-specific logic via `file_write` instead of regenerating ~500-800 tokens of boilerplate per run. U: I am thinking of templating mobile apps for future runs to be able to use less tokens for mobile app generation, but don't know how exactly should it be constructed Analyzed the existing template system (`AppTemplate` interface, `getTemplateById()`, template JSON files with nodes/edges/prompts) and determined it has zero concept of file scaffolds. Proposed a minimal additive change: `scaffoldPath` property in templates, backend copy-on-creation, and a system prompt update telling agents to use `directory_read` then write only app-specific code. No implementation done — design stage only.

compartment.title: Mobile app scaffold templates

compartment: Created two mobile app templates (Android + iOS) with scaffold directories of boilerplate files (build configs, manifests, stubs) that are copied to targetPath at schema creation — keeping boilerplate out of LLM generation to reduce token waste. Added `templateId` field to `CreateAppRequest`, `copyScaffold()` method in AppController that walks `templates/{id}/scaffold/` and copies files preserving relative structure, and scaffold file entries in frontend template definitions. Android scaffold: 6 files (build.gradle.kts, settings.gradle.kts, gradle.properties, app/build.gradle.kts, AndroidManifest.xml, MainActivity.kt). iOS scaffold: 3 files (Package.swift, AxolotlApp.swift, ContentView.swift). Pipeline: Source → Review → Agent → Verifier (gradlew/swift build) → Output. Commits 295a6feb, 1693. No outcome text required — the agent scaffold directories, backend copyScaffold method, and pipeline templates speak for themselves.

compartment.title: Provider model pre-fetch on startup and test-button health check

compartment: Added `ConcurrentHashMap` provider cache (5min TTL) to LlmService with `@EventListener(ApplicationReadyEvent)` startup pre-fetch of all Connected providers. Replaced SettingsController stub health check with real HTTP ping via `LlmService.checkProviderHealth()`. Frontend: settingsStore providers cache with `fetchProviders()`, `getModelsForProvider()`, `getAllModelOptions()`; App.vue triggers pre-fetch on mount; SettingsView test button shows green `"Connected (N models)"` badge or red `"No key or unavailable"` with error text, updating model list from live response. U: update available models list by provider on startup for adaptors that are in status 'Connected' and on clicking test-btn Both triggers implemented: startup via ApplicationReadyEvent pre-fetches all providers, test button calls the live health check endpoint.

compartment.title: Model enable/disable toggles with filtered dropdowns

compartment: Added `disabledModels` field to GraphProviderSetting with Neo4j persistence, SettingsController GET/PUT endpoints per provider, and frontend checkboxes per model in SettingsView that toggle `.disabledModels`. Updated AgentNode.vue, SchemaBuilderNode.vue, QuickStartDialog.vue, and PlanningModelsPicker.vue to filter `p.models` by `p.disabledModels` in dropdown iterations. The store's `getAllModelOptions()` and `getModelsForProvider()` exclude disabled models. Backward compatible — null/empty `disabledModels` shows all models. U: enable/disable models that are currently shown as selectable from provider. change every model dropdown list so that it only shows enabled models from available providers Implemented both aspects: toggle UI in SettingsView, filtering in all 4 model dropdown components across the app.

compartment.title: Listed available Zen models

compartment: Fetched and displayed all 41 Zen models from the live API, grouped by family: Free tier (6 models including big-pickle), Claude (8), Gemini (2), GPT-5 (17), GLM (2), MiniMax (2), Kimi (2), Qwen (2).

compartment.title: Added deepseek-v4-flash to Zen model list

compartment: Added `deepseek-v4-flash` to the hardcoded Zen model constant and fixed LlmService routing to match `deepseek-v4` prefix to the Zen provider (inserted before the general `deepseek-` → DeepSeek rule) so the model routes correctly.

compartment.title: Dynamic Zen model list from API

compartment: Replaced the hardcoded `ZEN_MODELS` static array in OpencodeZenProvider with a live `GET /v1/models` API call in `listModels()`. Removed the stale hardcoded list (was already missing models and used different naming conventions). Kept `FALLBACK_ZEN_MODELS` static list for when the API is unreachable. Models now auto-sync with Zen's offerings — no manual updates needed. U: the model list shouldn't be hardcoded Replaced the static array with a runtime API call; the hardcoded list is now only a fallback.

compartment.title: Removed all hardcoded model lists with Neo4j persistence

compartment: Eliminated every hardcoded model list across all providers. AnthropicProvider, DeepSeekProvider, OpenAiProvider, and OpencodeZenProvider now all fetch model lists dynamically via their respective `GET /v1/models` API endpoints. The static `FALLBACK_ZEN_MODELS` in OpencodeZenProvider was removed entirely — no provider has a hardcoded fallback. Added `@Property("models") List<String> models` to GraphProviderSetting, `getModels(name)`/`setModels(name, list)` to SettingsService (compares before write to avoid unnecessary Neo4j updates), and wired LlmService to persist models on every successful API fetch (startup preload, test button, refresh, getProvidersInfo) and fall back to Neo4j on API failure. RlmProvider exempted by design (no API endpoint). U: there still are hardcoded model lists. I want it only to be dynamic, refreshed on each axolotl start and on each provider test for available providers (previous list could be stored in db and redrawn only if there are changes, model selection also has to be stored in db) Drove the complete elimination of hardcoded model lists and the addition of dynamic fetching + Neo4j persistence across all providers.

compartment.title: Settings panel UI/UX overhaul

compartment: Implemented 6 UX improvements to the Settings view: (1) unavailable providers collapse to header-only with expand chevron and animated rotate; (2) model search filter input with magnifying glass icon that filters checkboxes by model name in real-time; (3) model checkboxes grouped by family (Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek, Qwen, MiniMax, Kimi, GLM, Free, Open Source, Other) with uppercase small-cap group labels; (4) Enable All / Disable All buttons at the top of each provider's model section; (5) green/red status pill badge replacing the inline `status-text` span; (6) visual dividers between settings fields and model toggles. Frontend compiles clean with zero TypeScript errors.

compartment.title: Settings panel UX improvements: test-unsaved, collapse, delete confirm

compartment: Reviewed SettingsView and identified 7 issues. Implemented the 3 highest-impact fixes per user approval: (1) test button now passes unsaved apiKey/baseUrl as optional query params to `GET /{provider}/health` so it tests the typed credentials, not the saved ones; (2) custom endpoints collapse with chevron animation matching built-in providers; (3) two-click delete confirmation on custom endpoint trash icon. Followed up with model scrollbox max-height increased from 240px to 360px, `.confirm-delete` CSS style, dead `isNew` code removal, and fixed LlmServiceTest compilation error (needed SettingsService constructor param). Frontend compiles clean, backend restarts successfully.

compartment.title: Persisted reusable session patterns as agent memories

compartment: Following user directive to capture reusable patterns from this discussion, saved two code patterns into project memory for agent reuse: two-click inline delete confirmation pattern (for delete actions needing confirmation without modals) and test-before-save via @RequestParam pattern (for features where users test config before persisting). Also verified backend health endpoint works with the new optional apiKey/baseUrl query params. Session summary delivered documenting all 3 completed Settings UX improvements.

compartment.title: Model toggle collapsible groups + batch toggle boolean fix

compartment: Gave a design review of the 44-model flat checkbox list identifying 5 problems (scroll-fodder layout, invisible group hierarchy, no bulk actions, no state summary, silent truncation) with 6 recommendations. Implemented collapsible group headers with sticky positioning, per-provider count badges ("6 / 8"), group-level All/None buttons on hover, search bar that auto-expands matching groups, and a summary footer ("36 / 40 models enabled"). Removed the 360px max-height scrollbox constraint. Wrote 5 Playwright tests. Discovered and fixed a pre-existing inverted boolean bug: `setModelDisabled(provider, model, disabled)` takes `disabled=true` to mean "add to disabled list", but all 4 batch All/None functions (enableAllInGroup, disableAllInGroup, enableAllModels, disableAllModels) passed the raw value without inverting — `enableAllInGroup` passed `true` (disabling instead of enabling) and `disableAllInGroup` passed `false` (enabling instead of disabling). Same bug in the top-level All/None buttons predated this refactor. All 29 Playwright tests pass.

compartment.title: BlockConfigPanel dynamic model options

compartment: Replaced the 6 hardcoded `<option>` elements in BlockConfigPanel with dynamic grouped options fetched from `settingsApi.getProviders()`, filtering out `disabledModels`. Added `onMounted` fetch, computed `providerOptionsByGroup`, and `<optgroup>` rendering — same pattern as AgentNode.vue. The dropdown now respects model enable/disable toggles from Settings. BlockConfigPanel was the last component with hardcoded model options. All 29 Playwright tests pass.

compartment.title: Schema persistence on navigation away and back

compartment: Fixed schema changes not persisting after navigating from Studio to Dashboard and back. Two interacting causes: (1) `onActivated` skipped re-fetch when returning to the same schema ID, using stale in-memory cache; (2) `saveConfig()` was fire-and-forget (no `await`) so in-flight saves could be lost on navigation. Fixed `onActivated` to always re-fetch the schema from `schemaApi.getSchema(currentId)`, falling back to in-memory cache on network error. Reverted an interim async-save change after recognizing that making `saveConfig` async on `@input` (which fires on every keystroke) would cause typing lag — the `onActivated` re-fetch alone covers all persistence gaps.

compartment.title: ResumeBanner empty schemaId guard and session cleanup

compartment: Added early-return guard in ResumeBanner.vue when `schemaId` is empty, preventing the double-slash URL `/schemas//runs/paused` and its harmless 404. Verified PlanningModelsPicker and BlockConfigPanel both use the dynamic provider pattern correctly. Saved session summary to memory documenting 3 completed changes: BlockConfigPanel dynamic models, StudioView onActivated re-fetch, and ResumeBanner guard. All 29 Playwright tests pass.

compartment.title: Neo4j deprecation warning suppression

compartment: Suppressed the `Neo.ClientNotification.Statement.FeatureDeprecationWarning` spam from Spring Data Neo4j 6.3.x auto-generated `id()` Cypher. Two-punch fix: (1) driver-level `NotificationConfig.disableCategories(Set.of(NotificationCategory.DEPRECATION))` in Neo4jConfig.java prevents the notification at the protocol level; (2) `org.springframework.data.neo4j.cypher.deprecation: ERROR` log level in application.yml as second defense. Verified all deprecation WARN messages are gone from logs after provider health check triggers the GraphProviderSetting query. All 29 Playwright tests pass.

compartment.title: Full persistence round-trip bug fix and Playwright test

compartment: Debugged and fixed a 3-part persistence round-trip bug discovered while writing a Playwright persistence test. **Edge sync**: BlueprintView `onConnect` created edges in VueFlow state but never synced them to `schemaStore.currentSchema` — fixed by adding `schemaStore.updateSchema()` call in `onConnect` (and its internal connect helper). **Save path mapping**: BlockConfigPanel saved `prompt: prompt.value` but `NodeData.java` declares `systemPrompt` — Jackson silently dropped `prompt` during backend deserialization. Fixed to save `systemPrompt` in 3 places. **Load path mapping**: After API reload, BlueprintView maps `n.data?.config` as node data so values live at `node.value.data.*` top-level, but load code only read from `node.value.data?.config.*`. Fixed load to try top-level `data.*` first, fall back to `config.*` nesting. SchemaStore sync now saves values in both `config` (for VueFlow active editing) and top-level (for backend NodeData). Wrote a Playwright persistence test that creates a schema with Source+Agent nodes, connects edge, selects model, fills prompt, verifies via API, navigates to Dashboard and back, then verifies all three persisted. All 30 tests pass in 52s. Committed.

compartment.title: QuickStartDialog Generate button default model fix

compartment: Fixed the Generate button using `provider.defaultModel` (`deepseek-v4-flash`) instead of the user's chosen default model (`big-pickle`). The model selector already existed in QuickStartDialog but defaulted to the provider's built-in default. Added `settingsApi.getUserDefaultModel()` call in `loadProviders()` — the dropdown now defaults to the user's preference, falling back to provider default only if no user default is set. Updated the QuickStartDialog test mock to include `getUserDefaultModel`. All 30 e2e tests + 10 unit tests pass. Committed.

compartment.title: Suggested prompt for emotion tracking app pipeline

compartment: Read `.ideas/002.md` (Russian-language emotion tracking app spec — mood diary with 6 emotion categories, calendar analytics, tips, dark/light theme, offline-first). Suggested two prompt approaches: a Quick Start prompt describing the pipeline structure (4-node: source→review→agent→verifier→output) vs. directly describing the app. The actual app code gets written when the pipeline executes, not during schema creation.

compartment.title: Created Бережно schema via browser

compartment: Opened Axolotl Web UI in headed browser, logged in via localStorage token injection, navigated to Quick Start dialog, typed "Создай пайплайн для генерации приложения-дневника эмоций..." prompt with app name "Бережно", clicked Generate, verified schema appeared on Dashboard with correct name and target path. Schema creation via browser-based Quick Start flow.

compartment.title: Set up Бережно pipeline with design doc content

compartment: User confirmed the deepseek-v4-flash-free model selection for the Бережно emotion diary app pipeline. Added the `.ideas/002.md` design document content to the Source node via browser automation. Encountered a Vue reactivity issue where browser-evaluated text assignment didn't trigger Vue's reactive system — worked around by saving content via direct API call and creating `scripts/update_berechno_schema.sh` as a reusable update helper. The 5-node pipeline (Source→Review→Agent→Verifier→Output) is ready for execution.

compartment.title: Investigated Receive block input mode design

compartment: Analyzed the Receive block's current implementation across frontend and backend. Found a frontend-backend disconnect: the Input Type dropdown in BlockConfigPanel.vue changes cosmetic node type labels (`source`, `source-file`, `source-webhook`, `source-schedule`) but doesn't control actual behavior. The backend `handleSourceNode` in NodeExecutor.java already supports `text`, `memory`, `url`, and `project` source types via `config.sourceType`. Proposed enhancing with real input modes: Direct Text (existing), File Reference (new — reads file at execution time), URL Fetch / Project Directory (existing in backend but not surfaced). No implementation done.

compartment.title: Receive Block source types, backend file reader, Schema Properties Panel

compartment: Designed and implemented three features based on user's input. BlockConfigPanel.vue: Input Type dropdown now controls `config.sourceType` (text, file, url, project) with conditional input sections — file reference path (relative to schema targetPath) + browse icon, URL fetch input, project directory with depth/max files. ExecutionUtilityService.java: new `"file"` sourceType handler resolves relative paths against schema targetPath with 1MB size limit and descriptive Russian error messages. SchemaPropertiesPanel.vue (new) + BlueprintView.vue wiring: clicking empty canvas shows editable schema name, description, read-only target path with folder icon, default model selector, and Add Node/Run/Quick Start buttons. Replaced all emoji icons with inline SVGs per user instruction. Committed e4348107 (7 files, +828/-23). Design file confirmed as fully implemented with all open questions resolved. U: We stopped using emojis, remember it and go on Replaced all emoji icons with inline SVGs across BlockConfigPanel.vue and SchemaPropertiesPanel.vue during the implementation pass.

compartment.title: Dashboard apps-section: recently opened tracking, collapsible sections, 24h time

compartment: Implemented Dashboard apps-section improvements. Tracked recently opened apps in localStorage (key `axolotl_recent_apps`, max 5) — `openApp()`, `createFromTemplate()`, and `createBlankApp()` all record visits. Replaced the flat app grid with two collapsible sections: Recent (expanded by default, up to 5 recently opened) and All Apps (collapsed by default, both sorted by `updatedAt` descending). SVG chevrons animate rotation on collapse. Count badges on each section header. When searching, sections disappear and show flat filtered list. AppCard "Updated" date now shows 24h:minutes format. Committed 952fef32 (2 files, +209/-14). U: on 'Updated' it would be nice to also have time in 24h:minutes format Added 24h:minutes time to AppCard's formatDate() function.

compartment.title: Fixed Quick Start generating agent nodes instead of review/verifier types

compartment: Found that PROMPT_TO_SCHEMA_SYSTEM template in SchemaService.java only listed source, agent, output, condition, loop, memory, guardrail, transform — missing review and verifier node types. When users described review/verify steps in Quick Start prompts, the LLM fell back to generic agent nodes since it didn't know the correct types exist. Added two lines to include review and verifier types. Both frontend (custom blocks in palette) and backend (handlers in NodeRouter) already fully support these types. Committed 96f21730. Next Quick Start generation will output correct node types for review/verify pipeline descriptions.

compartment.title: Proposal: Quick Start presets and dashboard option

compartment: Suggested turning the previously used Quick Start prompt into a Quick Start template and adding Quick Start as an option on the Dashboard New App flow. The assistant proposed adding a small set of preset prompts (emotion diary, chatbot, content generator, game, etc.) to QuickStartDialog via a dropdown that loads the textarea for editing, leaving the rest of the UI unchanged.

compartment.title: Implemented Quick Start presets, Dashboard integration, and related UI/model work

compartment: Implemented Quick Start presets and Dashboard integration and pushed feature commits (0e759304, 47b4ea40). Changes include: QuickStartDialog presets (4) and a create mode that accepts an app name and generates a schema; a Quick Start button in the Dashboard header that opens QuickStartDialog and, on schema creation, pushes the new schema into the store and redirects the user into Studio; dynamic model-provider lists populated from the provider API with persistence in Neo4j; model enable/disable toggles in Settings that filter model dropdowns across AgentNode, SchemaBuilderNode, BlockConfigPanel, PlanningModelsPicker, and QuickStartDialog; Settings UI overhaul (collapsible model groups, search, All/None); Studio now re-fetches schemas when activated; ResumeBanner guards against empty schemaId (double-slash) redirects; and Neo4j `id()` deprecation warnings are suppressed via NotificationConfig. TypeScript compiles clean and the frontend updates were committed and pushed.

compartment.title: Design: what presets should describe — app logic vs pipeline

compartment: User observed presets currently describe app logic rather than Axolotl pipeline structure and asked whether that is correct. The assistant explained that the LLM needs both kinds of information (what the app should do so node systemPrompts can be populated, plus a pipeline topology so nodes are created correctly) and recommended keeping both but improving format: make presets explicitly structured with separate APP: and PIPELINE: sections, and optionally offer pipeline-template choices (chat vs content-generation) as the next step. The assistant asked whether to switch to a pipeline-first preset format or keep the mixed-but-structured approach.
ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS: * QuickStartDialog exposes preset prompts plus a create mode that accepts an app name and emits @add-to-canvas with the created schema. * Dashboard header includes a Quick Start button that opens QuickStartDialog. * Model-provider lists are populated dynamically from the provider API and persisted in Neo4j; Settings expose enable/disable toggles that filter model dropdowns across AgentNode, SchemaBuilderNode, BlockConfigPanel, PlanningModelsPicker, and QuickStartDialog. * Studio re-fetches schemas on activation to ensure schema persistence is up to date. * QuickStart presets include both an APP description and a PIPELINE structure because the LLM needs both to populate node systemPrompts and choose pipeline topology.
ENVIRONMENT: * Neo4j id() deprecation warnings are suppressed via NotificationConfig.
WORKFLOW_RULES: * When Quick Start creates a schema, push the created schema into the store and navigate to Studio for that schema.
